BDI_II.responses: {"Q0":"0. I do not feel sad.","Q1":"0. I am not discouraged about my future.","Q2":"1. I have failed more than I should have.","Q3":"0. I get as much pleasure as I ever did from the things I enjoy.","Q4":"1. I feel guilty over many things I have done or should have done.","Q5":"0. I don't feel I am being punished.","Q6":"0. I feel the same about myself as ever.","Q7":"0. I don't criticize or blame myself more than usual.","Q8":"0. I don't have any thoughts of killing myself.","Q9":"0. 11.response: {"Q0":"Judging by the game itself something to do with quick decision making. But the questions indicate something about the topic of the OCF disorder however, I don't know how the data collected by this game could be used in that area."}

0.response: {"Q0":"For the galaxy choice I picked one and kept looking up/down depending on which galaxy I picked. The planets had the exact same pattern of tools so I just had to pay attention to their color so I didn't have to look at the 'obstacles' (dirt rock or wood). I looked up/down because then I could focus on the area where the arrow for my picked galaxy would appear making it easier to click it in time."}

7.response: {"Q0":"I was hoping they would give me more gems then they did but it didn't impact my motivation."}

9.response: {"Q0":"I paid more attention to he galaxies as they didn't have a fixed pattern as the planets, so they required my attention each time I had to make a choice. The plantets I just memorised"}

1.response: {"Q0":"Well from the very begining I ignored the color yellow as there were no gems to be found there. Later on I started to ignore red as well because the astronaut flew away."}

2.response: {"Q0":"I didn't really pay attention to that but maybe around 40% of the time?"}

8.response: {"Q0":"Basically I tried to do a proportion calculation to determine which option was more profitable with it's chance of happening. (so 1$ with 50% is better than 7% with 10% but not better than 7$ with 30%)"}
